channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Buitreraptor
item.description: Source: here. The discovery of a remarkably complete dromeosaur from the 90 million year old Cretaceous beds of Patagonia, Buitreraptor, is bring to fore many issues that concern the events close to the emergence of birds. As the only group of dinosaurs that survived the K-T extinction event the birds have received intense attention. But now it is becoming clear that most of their well-known adaptations were already present in various forms in the theropod dinosaurs from which they arose.

item.title: Ramblings
item.description: Source: here. Today I was at the Philadelphia museum- a few childhood dreams of mine were finally fulfilled. I got an opportunity to examine the following skulls and skeletons at some length: The skull Eoraptor The skull of Hererrasaurus - both of which are amongst the earliest theropods. The skeletons of Dromeosaurus and Saurornithoides, two deinonychosaurs close to the origins of birds The second skeleton of Tyrannosaurus rex (this specimen has a better preserved fore limb that allowed me study carpal and ungual morphology)
